title: Why second-hand shopping is growing
explanation:
Why second-hand shopping is growing

Buying second-hand clothes and furniture used to be seen by some people as a last resort. Today, it has become much more popular, especially among younger consumers. Some shoppers want to save money, while others are trying to reduce waste and avoid fast fashion. Online marketplaces have also made it easier to find unusual items in good condition. For many people, second-hand shopping now feels both practical and environmentally responsible.

questions.text: Second-hand shopping is especially popular among [*younger consumers|retired doctors|small children].
People buy second-hand items partly to [*save money|travel faster|learn history].
Online marketplaces make it easier to find [*unusual items|new food|jobs].
The text links second-hand shopping with [*reducing waste|more pollution|higher salaries].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Second-hand shopping used to be seen as a last ____.  | *resort | minute | chance sale
questions.feedback: That phrase appears in the text.

questions.text: Some people want to avoid fast ____.  | *fashion | service | shopping bag
Items can often be found in good ____.  | *condition | salary | attendance
For many people, second-hand shopping is practical and environmentally ____.  | *responsible | delayed | formal
The passage mainly explains [*reasons for a trend|how to open a shop|why furniture is expensive].
questions.feedback: It explains why second-hand shopping is growing.

questions.text: The text mentions both [*financial and environmental motives|only fashion advice|only charity work].
The trend is described as [*more common than before|almost finished|limited to one city].
questions.feedback: It has become much more popular.
